title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, and Invalidation · Transfer Build
summary: An independent TypeScript team receives different tenants, browsers, file sizes, media, regions, compliance, traffic, and cost constraints and transfers cloudfront cache keys, origin requests, and invalidation into a new cache identity and freshness contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of cache identity and freshness contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Cache Key Identity
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9281 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to align typed cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, and release freshness; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Cache Key Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Cache Key Identity, keep Cache Key Identity connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Audit the initial evidence packet · Lab Compression Vary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7662 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to align typed cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, and release freshness; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Compression Vary
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-compression-vary-1x6pdf" that uses case 6444 to align typed cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, and release freshness. Independent transfer: choose the fixture, defend the invariant, and document a counterexample without scaffold. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
